consignor.invoice_value:
Bakels Sweden AB
von Utfallsgatan 26-30
SE-40252 Gothenburg
Sweden

rules_applied.outcome: Consignor name was explicitly found in the invoice issuer/address/footer text.
Invoice identifies the consignor as Swedish and no contradictory country appears.

consignor.motivation: The certificate consignor legal name 'Bakels Sweden AB' is explicitly present in the invoice. The invoice issuer/footer address shows the same legal name and country Sweden. No contradictory company identity or country was found. This satisfies consignor verification under 4.1.0, 4.1.4 and 4.1.5.

rules_applied.outcome: Consignee was explicitly found in the invoice delivery/business address blocks.

consignee.motivation: The certificate consignee 'Parskak' with address in Tehran, Iran is explicitly present in the invoice delivery address and business address blocks. Minor formatting differences such as 'P.O. Box' versus 'P.O.Box' and the added postal fragment '0000' do not contradict identity. Country Iran matches exactly. MATCH under 4.2.0.1, 4.2.3 and 4.2.4.

225721 Jilk 60

225715 Jilk 60
Hs no:21069098 Origin: SWE
SEGREGATED CU-RSPO SCC-827455
40 Pallets = Bakery Ingredients
Container No: MIOU 505910-8
Seal: 9418565

rules_applied.outcome: No article number was stated in the certificate, so verification proceeded to controlled text match.
The identity-bearing designation 'Jilk 60' from the certificate is explicitly present in the invoice.
Additional logistics text such as pallet, container and seal information does not alter product identity.

goods_description.motivation: The certificate does not provide an article number, so Priority 1 was not applicable. Using controlled text match, the identity-bearing product designation 'Jilk 60' can be identified exactly in the invoice line descriptions. The remaining certificate text consists of supplementary logistics information (40 pallets, container number MIOU 505910-8, seal 9418565) that is also supported by the invoice and does not contradict the product identity. MATCH under 4.3.3 and 4.3.4.

rules_applied.outcome: Certificate weight values include explicit unit and weight category.
Multiple certificate quantity values are allowed; no contradiction exists in the invoice.
Invoice total net and gross weights match the certificate exactly.

quantity.motivation: The certificate quantity field explicitly states '23 552 Kg net' and '25 150 Kg gross'. The invoice repeats the same total net and gross weights verbatim. Both numeric values, units and weight categories are explicit and consistent, so the quantity control point is a direct MATCH under 4.4.2.2, 4.4.3.1 and 4.4.4.

rules_applied.outcome: Country name Sweden and ISO code SWE were normalized as the same country.

origin.motivation: The certificate box 3 states origin 'Sweden'. The invoice line items explicitly state 'Origin: SWE'. ISO country-code normalization is allowed for origin comparison, and no contradictory origin appears. MATCH under 4.5.1, 4.5.4 and 4.5.5.

overall_assessment.decision_summary: Document A is a Certificate of Origin and Document B is an invoice. No LC reference was identified, so normal verification rules were applied. Document pairing is supported because the certificate consignor and consignee are both explicitly present in the invoice. All applicable control points—consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity and origin—matched without contradiction. The document pair is therefore IDENTICAL and eligible for auto approval.
